title: SACRAMENTO—Assemblymember James C. Ramos (D-Highland) today announced he is strengthening health and clean air protections in his bill, AB 1748, regarding siting of local warehouse
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The 2023 Advanced Clean Fleets regulation contributes to the transition to zero-emission vehicles.",
    "The South Coast Air Quality Management District's Warehouse Indirect Source Rule contributes to reducing emissions caused by the movement of goods.",
    "The bill AB 1748 adds a zero emission equipment requirement for construction.",
    "The bill AB 1748 establishes air quality standards for construction equipment.",
    "The bill AB 1748 encourages local agencies to plan truck routes away from sensitive receptors.",
    "The bill AB 1748 limits idling during construction for heavy construction equipment.",
    "The bill AB 1748 includes measures to decrease idling with cold storage electric requirements at loading docks.",
    "The bill AB 1748 complies with Warehouse Actions and Investments to Reduce Emissions Points under the South Coast Air Quality Management District’s Rule 2305.",
    "The bill AB 1748 complies with the 2023 Advanced Clean Fleets regulation, which is part of CARB’s strategy to transition to zero-emission medium- and heavy-duty vehicles.",
    "The implementation of the bill AB 1748 is delayed to ensure compliance with the measures in the bill.",
    "The bill AB 1748 is set to terminate in 2030 in anticipation of updates to CARB’s Air Quality and Land Use Handbook."
  ]
}
```
